rulings.Case Title: JACK BESSIRE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P-AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS B et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 16, 2007, LINE 1 DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR, REMOVAL TO FEDERAL COURT ON 3/20/07. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: GURSON S. DANG VS. MADELEINE MURPHY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 16, 2007, LINE 2 DEFENDANT SARAH ANN HALDAN'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES ACTS BY HALDAN IN FURTHERANCE OF HER OWN FINANCIAL GAIN, WHICH GIVES RISE TO A DUTY TO PLAINTIFF UNDER ALLIED EQUIPMENT V. LITTON 7 CAL 4TH 503, 511, 521. IT ALSO ALLEGES THE TORT OF INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS AND A CONSPIRACY IN WHICH HALDAN PARTICIPATED TO INTERFERE WITH PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED CONTRACTUAL RIGHT OF FIRST REFUSAL. ALLIED EQUIPMENT AT 513. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: NATALIE PERTSOVSKY VS. PETER CHASE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 16, 2007, LINE 4 DEFENDANT MICHAEL MA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BUDEN OF PROOF BASED ON THE DEFENSE OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, CCP 340.5. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THAT ISSUE. THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT THE INJURY OCCURRED ON OR BEFORE JULY 9, 2002, AS A CONSEQUENCE OF PLAINTIFF'S TREATMENT IN JUNE 2002. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE 3 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE COURT FINDS THE FOLLOWING FACTS PRESENTED BY DEFENDANT TO BE UNDISPUTED. 1;2;3;4 THAT DR. MA SAW PLAINTIFF ON JUNE 12, 2002 AND TREATED PLINTIFF AS DESCRIBED IN PLAINTIFF'S DISPUTED FACT 4;5 THAT DR MA TREATED PLAINTIFF ON JUNE 28, 2002 AND "CUT PIECE OF HER GUM'S FLESH TO ELONGATE ANCHOR TEETH #27, #28 AND #32. "AND PLUNGED THREAD AROUND EACH TOOTH TO PUSH GUM AWAY FROM THE TOOTH; 6;7;8;11;12;13;14;16;19. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: DAO LIEN VS. INSTITUTE ON AGING-ADULT DAY SERVICES et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 16, 2007, LINE 10 DEFENDANT INSTITUE ON AGING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION ONLY PLEADS FACTS ESTABLISHING NEGLIGENCE, NOT CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVEIDENCE OF RECKLESSNESS. THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION MUST ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP AND/OR ACTUAL FRAUD. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 1877
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Confirm First Supplemental Decision And Award
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 16, 2007, LINE 12 RESPONDENT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 1877'S Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Confirm First Supplemental Decision And Award. ALTHOUGH THE ARBITRATOR'S FAILURE TO ALLOW DISCOVERY COUPLED WITH CERTAIN RULINGS RAISE QUESTIONS ABOUT PROPOSED AWARD, THERE DOES NOT APPEAR A BASIS FOR DENYING THE MOTION. THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO CONFIRM THE AWARD. =(302/PJM/AP)
